faqs.question: How do I know if I have a pantry pest infestation in my Passaic home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small moths flying around your kitchen, tiny beetles in flour or grain products, webbing in food packages, small holes in packaging, and larvae or cocoons in cupboards. You may also notice a stale or unusual odor coming from stored foods. Our Passaic technicians can perform a thorough inspection to identify the extent of the problem.

faqs.question: What does your pantry pest control service in Passaic include?
faqs.answer: Our service includes a complete inspection of all food storage areas, identification of pest species, removal of infested materials, treatment of affected areas with safe insecticides, installation of pheromone traps, and sealing of entry points. We also provide detailed prevention guidance and follow-up visits to ensure complete elimination.

faqs.question: Are the treatments safe for my family and pets in Passaic?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-approved products specifically designed for food storage areas that are safe when applied correctly. Our technicians take extra precautions in kitchens and pantries, and we can discuss organic or low-toxicity options if you have specific concerns. Most treatments require minimal disruption to your daily routine.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to pantry pest problems in Passaic, NJ?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service for most Passaic locations. Once you contact us, we can typically schedule an appointment within 24 hours. The initial treatment takes 1-2 hours depending on the severity of the infestation, and most customers see significant reduction in pest activity within the first week.
